title: DHS Shutdown Continues Into Day 41 as Senate Negotiations Remain Deadlocked
detail: The partial DHS government shutdown extended into its 41st day on March 26, 2026, with Senate negotiations over funding remaining deadlocked. Democrats continued to demand ICE accountability measures including mandatory body cameras, identification requirements, judicial warrant standards, and restrictions on enforcement near sensitive locations. Republicans proposed restoring most DHS funding while keeping ICE's Enforcement and Removal Operations (ERO) largely intact using billions already authorized under the One Big Beautiful Bill Act. ICE had continued functioning through the shutdown using those emergency funds, while TSA workers remained unpaid and staffing shortages worsened.

title: ICE Detention Population Hits Record 73,000 — 84% Increase Since January 2025
detail: As of late March 2026, approximately 73,000 immigrants were held in ICE detention — the highest number ever recorded and an 84 percent increase from approximately 39,000 at the start of the Trump administration. Over 70 percent of detainees had no criminal record, and 95 percent had never been convicted of a violent crime. Congress had authorized $45 billion in new detention funding under the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, with analysts warning the system could more than triple in size over four years. ICE was using 104 more facilities than at the start of 2025, a 91 percent increase. 2025 was the deadliest year on record for ICE custody, with 2026 tracking to surpass it.

media.caption: ICE detention population reaches record 73,000 — 84% increase since January 2025
